surface.name: Developer Hub app + Access Token + POST /contacts
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Create a contact. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Intercom setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create a contact via REST API
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The REST API returns a successful response containing the new contact ID.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Create a contact.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Developer Hub app + Access Token + POST /contacts surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: An Intercom workspace and Developer Hub access.
A terminal with cURL or another HTTPS client.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Intercom and open Developer Hub for a workspace.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create an app and copy its access token.
primary_path.output: An app access token is available.
primary_path.success_signal: An app access token is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: POST the documented contact payload to /contacts with the access token and required headers.
primary_path.output: Intercom returns the created contact object.
primary_path.success_signal: Intercom returns the created contact object.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Confirm the response contains a contact ID and the submitted identifying data.
primary_path.output: A new contact ID is visible in the successful response.
primary_path.success_signal: A new contact ID is visible in the successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: The user must have permission to create an app in the target Intercom workspace.
The access token must be authorized for the contact operation.

sources.title: Getting started with an integration
evidence_supported: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
The REST API returns a successful response containing the new contact ID.

sources.title: Create a contact
evidence_supported: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
The REST API returns a successful response containing the new contact ID.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Create a contact path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
